steps.description: @assertion there's create and view on github button. and ui has no error
Click the "Create" button for the "Microsoft Foundry Agent" sample in the "Featured samples" section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension for Visual Studio Code.
@assertion sample preview pictures showed. and no error found.
Click the "Platform capability" dropdown filter in the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Tab" checkbox option in the "Capability" dropdown filter within the Samples gallery of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Language" dropdown menu in the "Samples" section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code, opening a filter list for selecting agent types.
Click the checkbox for "Python" in the Language filter dropdown within the Samples gallery of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
Click the dropdown arrow in the "Technology" filter on the "Samples" page within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code, to display selectable technology options.
Click the checkbox for "SSO" under the "Technology" dropdown filter in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit Samples page within Visual Studio Code.
@assertion there's no sample item showed.
Click the checkbox option for "clear all" in the SSO dropdown filter within the "Samples" panel of Microsoft Visual Studio Code, to refine the displayed sample projects.
@assertion there's samples shows in gallery
Click the search input box in the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code, positioning the cursor to type a search query for sample projects.
Type text: 'abcdefgh' into the search input field located in the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.
@assertion there's no sample item showed.
Undo the text "abcdefgh" entered in the search bar within the Samples tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code using the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Z.
@assertion there's samples shows in gallery
Click the "Search" input field in the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code to focus on it and enable sample searching.
Click the "Close (Ctrl+W)" button on the "Samples" tab header in Visual Studio Code to close the tab.
Click the "View Samples" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit welcome sidebar of Visual Studio Code, as indicated by the highlighted crosshair.
@assertion there's create and view on github button. and ui has no error
Click the "Travel Agent" sample title in the "Featured samples" section of the "Samples" tab within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
@assertion there's sample page readme opened correctly, and picture shows correctly and no error.
Click the "Create" button in the Travel Agent sample interface within Visual Studio Code’s Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.

Click the "Default folder" dropdown in the "Choose the folder where your project root folder will be located" dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code workspace, specifically for setting the project directory path.
Click the "README.md" file in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code within the "TRAVEL-AGENT" workspace to select or open the file.
